scenario.context: Station 2 of the daily run: classify network overview and roles cleanly.
scenario.mission: You classify network segments, component roles, and services of a company network professionally.

station.profile: Office clients, server services, network segments, and baseline inventory

customerRequest.subject: Station 02: prepare a network overview for new colleagues
customerRequest.body: Please prepare a compact network overview for new team members. Focus: roles of clients, servers, and infrastructure, plus a traceable classification of the most important network segments.

questions.title: Differentiate client and server
questions.prompt: Which statement best describes the roles of client and server?

options.text: A client uses services, a server provides services centrally
options.rationale: This describes the typical role distribution in a corporate network.

options.text: Client and server are always the same device
options.rationale: This can happen, but it is not a general role description.

options.text: A switch replaces the tasks of a server
options.rationale: A switch does not provide typical server services.

feedback.correct: Correct: This cleanly separates the roles in principle.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: please distinguish usage and service provision clearly.

questions.title: Structure network segments sensibly
questions.prompt: Which statements help classify network segments in a usable way? (Multiple selections)

options.text: Consider workstation network, server area, and internet connectivity separately
options.rationale: This makes it easier to assign tasks and risks.

options.text: Record mobile and stationary clients separately
options.rationale: Usage type often affects requirements and support.

options.text: Document data center or server room separately
options.rationale: Central services require a clear classification.

options.text: Keep all devices without purpose in one catch-all list
options.rationale: Without structure, the insight decreases significantly.

options.text: Mix endpoints, infrastructure, and services together

feedback.correct: Correct: You structure the network by tasks and usage.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: please separate areas and roles more clearly.

questions.title: Path of a typical client request
questions.prompt: Arrange the flow of a simple service request sensibly.
items: The response reaches the client again
The server service processes the request
The switch transports the request in the LAN
Gateway or router is involved if needed
correctOrder: The switch transports the request in the LAN
Gateway or router is involved if needed
The server service processes the request
The response reaches the client again

feedback.correct: Correct: The communication path is structured cleanly in principle.
feedback.wrong: Not optimal: think from the client, and order transport, routing, and service cleanly.

questions.title: Capture baseline inventory
questions.prompt: At one site there are 18 workstation clients, 2 printers, 1 meeting display, and 1 access point. How many endpoints must at least be listed in the baseline inventory?

feedback.wrong: Please count all mentioned endpoints together.

questions.title: Network overview for new colleagues
questions.prompt: Explain in 4-6 sentences the difference between workstation network, server services, and network infrastructure.
questions.expectedOutline: Clear classification of roles, brief operational context, and clean technical terms.

feedback.correct: Correct: The overview is concise, technically sound, and practical.
feedback.wrong: Please separate roles, areas, and components more clearly.
